svg.title: Left bay — final build plan
svg.desc: Single-sheet build reference: front elevation with shelf heights and content plan, side cross-section showing cleat placement, build notes, cut list, and shopping list.
svg.text: 46" W × 65" H × 29.5" D · 4 new shelves below existing 65" high shelf · graduated heights

g.text: wok, kettle, flour, rice, big jugs
you'd rather have daily stuff at hand)
• Per shelf: 3 cleats = 4 cleat sets total
• 45° clip on front bottom of side cleats
• Side cleats: 2 screws each, anchors OK
1. Mark 4 shelf lines with painter's tape
2. Find & mark studs on all 3 walls
6. Install side cleats level w/ back
7. Iron-on edge banding to shelf fronts
drill + ⅛" pilot bit + driver bit
• Test your tallest cereal box BEFORE
• Iron-on edge banding hides the raw
cleats look like a board on the wall
• Use a 4-ft level on every cleat. Even
⅛" off compounds and looks bad
≈ $8 total · grab a 4th to be safe
Small can white paint (or use what you have)
Have store cut the plywood into:
Free or $1/cut at most stores. Bring this
sheet to the cutting desk — they'll know.
2 more shelves when you do the right bay.
→ 8 clips @ 45° on front bottom corners of side cleats
just the side cleats — back cleats stay square
3 boards × 8 ft = 24 ft (cutting close). 4 boards safer.
Depends on where your studs land.
